issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions _openInstance and _closeInstance in the dropdownMenu controller toggle visibility of the panel but never set or update aria‑expanded on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(inst.open)) when opening and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation button missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger’s aria‑expanded state is only set when the menu is toggled; on page load it is absent, violating the requirement that the attribute reflect the initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init after locating _trigger, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') so the closed state is announced.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close hides the drawer by removing CSS classes but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, leaving it focusable to AT even when invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and remove it in open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel not hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance sets hidden on the panel but does not set aria‑hidden="true", so the panel remains in the accessibility tree while hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After panel.setAttribute('hidden','') add setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and remove it in _openInstance.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult adds error messages but does not set aria‑invalid on the field when validation fails, breaking the required ARIA error signalling.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when !result.valid call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid','true') and remove it when valid.

issuesFound.title: Pricing changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates plan prices and UI elements but never writes to a live region, so users of assistive tech are unaware of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call liveRegion.announce(`Pricing switched to ${period}`) or a more specific message.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates lack live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._updateCountBadge changes the visible count but does not announce the new total via an aria‑live region, leaving screen‑reader users uninformed.
issuesFound.fix: After setting the badge text, update a hidden <div role="status"> with the count or call liveRegion.announce.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard step changes are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: multiStepWizard._goToStep navigates between steps and updates focus but does not write to a live region, so the step change is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: After moving focus, call liveRegion.announce(`Step ${targetIndex+1} of ${_steps.length}: ${entering.title}`).

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._close hides the suggestion list by adding hidden but does not set aria‑hidden="true", so the list remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close, after suggestionListRenderer.hide() add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-hidden','true') or set it on the list container.

fpTitles: Multi‑step wizard step changes are not announced

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions add/remove CSS classes and lock scrolling, but they never set or clear aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in close() add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls link to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button (`_trigger`) is never given an aria‑controls attribute that points to the drawer element (`_drawer`). Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After locating _trigger and _drawer, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer));` during mobileNav.init().

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() show or hide the panel but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance() add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in _closeInstance() add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button is given aria‑haspopup but never receives aria‑controls that references the associated panel’s id, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In register() after ensuring the panel has an id, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ triggers missing aria‑controls link to answer panels
issuesFound.explanation: Although the code ensures each trigger has an id and each panel an id, it does not set aria‑controls on the trigger to reference the panel, breaking the explicit relationship.
issuesFound.fix: In faqRegistry.parse() after generating panelId, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch (monthly/annual) does not set aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle (`_toggleEl`) toggles a CSS class but never updates aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded, leaving its state hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _onToggleClick() add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));` and similarly set aria‑expanded if appropriate.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() calls srUtils.markInvalid() which only adds aria‑describedby but never sets aria‑invalid="true". WCAG requires aria‑invalid to reflect validation state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate() after a failed validation, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and on success remove it with `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions (contactForm, newsletterForm) inherit the same missing aria‑invalid behavior
issuesFound.explanation: Both contactForm._onSubmit() and newsletterForm._onSubmit() rely on inlineValidator, so validation errors are not announced via aria‑invalid, violating ARIA best practices.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the fix from Issue 9 is applied globally; after inlineValidator.validate() adds aria‑invalid, the forms will automatically reflect the correct state.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not update aria‑expanded when suggestions panel opens/closes
issuesFound.explanation: The search input toggles the suggestion list visibility via suggestionListRenderer.show()/hide(), but never sets aria‑expanded to true/false, so screen readers cannot know when the autocomplete is open.
issuesFound.fix: In _ open() add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in _close() add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search toggle does not set aria‑controls to the suggestion list (already set) but also missing aria‑expanded initialisation on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The input receives aria-controls during suggestionListRenderer.mount(), but the initial aria‑expanded value is never set, leaving its default undefined.
issuesFound.fix: After mounting, set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` so the state is explicit before any interaction.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close via outside click does not reset aria‑expanded to false
issuesFound.explanation: _onOutsideClick() closes the drawer but does not clear aria‑expanded on the trigger, leaving it stuck on “true”.
issuesFound.fix: After close() call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu keyboard handling (Enter/Space) opens panel but does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In _dropdownMenu._onTriggerKeydown() the panel is opened via _openInstance() but the trigger’s aria‑expanded state is never updated.
issuesFound.fix: After _openInstance(inst) add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and similarly clear it in _closeInstance().

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new period via live region (only CSS updates)
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod() updates button classes and price text but does not send a live‑region announcement, so users of assistive tech are unaware of the billing period change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged?.replace('{period}', periodLabel));` or a similar message.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel open/close does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() change visibility but do not use liveRegion.announce() to inform screen readers that an answer was expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened?.replace('{title}', item.question));` and after closing, announce `STRINGS.faqClosed`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑toggle switch (`data‑billing-toggle`) missing `aria‑pressed` and never updates it
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch that flips between monthly/annual billing is a toggle button but does not expose its state via `aria‑pressed`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update `aria‑expanded` when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ uses custom `open()`/`close()` functions that add/remove CSS classes but never set `aria‑expanded` on the button, leaving the disclosure state hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `open()` and `close()` add:  
```javascript
setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);   // in open()
setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);  // in close()
```

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: The email `<input>` receives an error message but its `aria‑invalid` remains unset, so screen readers cannot detect the error.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on contact‑form fields (e.g., `contact-email`)
issuesFound.fix:
In the validation loop, for each field that fails, call `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name (`aria‑label`)
issuesFound.explanation: The button (`SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn`) is focusable but has no `aria‑label`, so screen‑reader users cannot know its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
After obtaining `_btn`, add:  
```javascript
setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.scrollToTopLabel || 'Scroll back to top');
```

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle switch missing `aria‑label` or `aria‑description` for its purpose
issuesFound.explanation: The switch element is interactive but provides no accessible name, making its purpose unclear.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.billingToggleLabel || 'Toggle billing period');
```

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer does not set `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()` add:  
```javascript
_setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);
```
  And in `open()` remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels are hidden via `hidden` attribute but the corresponding trigger never receives `aria‑expanded="false"` on page load (initial state)
issuesFound.fix:
During registration, after creating `inst`, set:  
```javascript
setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);
```

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing‑period change does not announce the new billing period to screen readers
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the UI, add:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(
  (STRINGS.billingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period switched to {period}')
    .replace('{period}', period)
);
```

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all interactive elements, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element is focused. outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule explicitly sets outline: none without restoring a visible outline. This removes the focus indicator specifically for buttons, failing WCAG 2.4.7. Relying on box-shadow elsewhere is not sufficient for High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0. While :focus has a box-shadow, :focus-visible (keyboard) does not, and box-shadow is not visible in High Contrast Mode. Keyboard users will lose the focus ring on the search input.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline on :focus-visible without providing a visible outline alternative. The box-shadow on :focus is not a valid replacement in High Contrast Mode. This blocks access for keyboard users filling out forms.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content fully visible on screen instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the intended functionality of hiding labels or text from sighted users while keeping them for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses text and significantly reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision. Negative word-spacing is not recommended for body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24x24 CSS pixels. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24x24 CSS pixels. Small buttons are hard to tap accurately for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px width and height. While above 24px, it is below the recommended 44px target size, making it difficult to activate on touch screens without zooming.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is sized to 20x20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24x24 CSS pixels. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to toggle the checkbox on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links use #94a3b8 text on a #555555 background. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.6:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision may not be able to read these links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and only use a color change on hover to indicate interactivity. This fails SC 1.4.1 (Use of Color) as color alone distinguishes the link state. Users with color blindness may not perceive them as links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in social links
issuesFound.explanation: The social links remove underlines and rely solely on color changes for hover states. This fails SC 1.4.1 as color alone distinguishes the link. Non-color indicators like underlines or icons are required.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the navigation is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2850: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This leaves the accessibility tree out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2872: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The register() function initializes aria-expanded="false", but it is never updated when the state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3054: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown panel, the function removes CSS classes and sets the hidden attribute but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3068: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function sets item.open = true and shows the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistency where the expanded state is only announced when closing, not when opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4822: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not update aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestions panel open function sets _searchState.open = true but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the search input element (which has role="combobox"). Screen readers will not announce that the suggestions list is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4232: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not update aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestions close function sets _searchState.open = false but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input element.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4252: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed or aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons ([data-filter-value]) indicate active state only via CSS classes. They must convey their pressed/selected state to assistive technologies using aria-pressed or aria-selected attributes.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~6283: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` (or 'aria-selected' if implementing tabs)

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function that marks form fields as invalid only updates aria-describedby to link the error message, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state of the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1882: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the existing `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function calls srUtils.markValid() which removes aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~7085: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` within the `if (result.valid)` block, after calling `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Since the navigation is closed by default, the initial state must be conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2925: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element acts as a combobox but is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. The suggestions panel is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4328: After `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden using CSS classes only (display/visibility changes) but does not receive aria-hidden="true" when closed. Screen readers may still perceive the off-screen navigation content.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2875: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after the drawer is visually hidden (or ensure the CSS hiding uses display:none which removes from a11y tree, but explicit aria-hidden is safer).

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates (showing/hiding cards), no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users of the result count or that the filter has been applied. Users are unaware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~6170: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/annual), the prices update visually but no announcement is made to screen reader users. Users are not informed that the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~5540: After `setPeriod(next);` in _onToggleClick, or within setPeriod after updating the display, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens or closes, no announcement is made to inform screen reader users of the state change, even though focus is not moved to the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2855 (in open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` Line ~2880 (in close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen reader users. While focus is managed, the context change should be announced for clarity.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it when the navigation opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the open navigation drawer into the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2852: After opening the drawer, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.fix:
Line (in dropdownMenu._openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the start of the function.
    Line (in dropdownMenu._closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the function.
    Line (in dropdownMenu.register): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not toggle aria-expanded when items are expanded or collapsed. While the attribute is set on initialisation, it is not updated during user interaction, causing screen readers to report stale state information.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in faqAccordion.open): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`
    Line (in faqAccordion.close): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not set aria-expanded="true" when the suggestion list opens or aria-expanded="false" when it closes. The attribute is also missing on initial page load, violating the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in siteSearch._open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the end of the function.
    Line (in siteSearch._close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the end of the function.
    Line (in siteSearch.init): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after wiring ARIA attributes.

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields fail validation, aria-invalid="true" is not set on the input element. When fields become valid, aria-invalid is not cleared. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in srUtils.markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`
    Line (in srUtils.markValid): Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after removing the describedby attribute.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, only CSS classes are removed; aria-hidden="true" is not applied to the drawer element. Screen readers can still access the visually hidden navigation content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in mobileNav.open): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
    Line (in mobileNav.close): Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
